name: Playwright QA
description: Automated visual QA testing using Playwright — navigate web apps like a real user, capture screenshots, find bugs, and fix them.
prompt:
Run automated visual QA on the web application at {{url}}.

If `server_command` is provided, start the dev server with `{{server_command}}` before QA begins.

Checks to run: {{checks}}

Workflow:
1. Use qa_run to start the full QA pipeline
2. Review the report for issues
3. Fix any critical or major issues found
4. Re-run qa_run to verify fixes
5. Call qa_cleanup when done

Provide a summary of all findings and fixes.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Target URL to test (defaults to http://localhost:3000)

parameters.name: server_command
parameters.description: Command to start the dev server (e.g., 'npm run dev')

parameters.name: checks
parameters.description: Comma-separated checks: visual_snapshot, console_errors, network_errors, interaction_test, responsive_check, accessibility_check, performance_check

routing.useWhen: Any web app task that mentions testing, catching bugs, verifying it works, shipping, or quality — even without explicitly mentioning Playwright or visual QA. Trigger on phrases like 'test it', 'catch bugs', 'before shipping', 'make sure it works', 'verify it', 'does it work', 'ship it', 'check for issues' alongside web app work.
routing.dontUseWhen: The task is purely about unit tests (jest/vitest), API testing, non-web application testing, or native mobile app testing.
routing.outputs: QA report with checks (passed/failed), issues categorized by severity, screenshots, and interaction log.
routing.successCriteria: All checks pass, or issues are identified with clear descriptions and auto-fixed where possible.

positive: Build a todo app in React, test it to catch any bugs before shipping
